name: _README
description: This file is a language-agnostic conformance SPEC for the nbus line protocol. It is pure data (no code) so a runner in ANY language can execute it against a real daemon. Each object after this one is a test vector. See tests/vectors.schema.md for the full schema. Vectors use bucket names unique to each vector so that shared daemon state (buckets/keys are process-global) does not cross-contaminate. Volatile/global assertions (STATS, BUCKETS) use regex `matches` rather than exact counts.

name: emit-sub-receives-event
description: conn1 SUBs an exact event; conn0 EMITs it; conn1 receives the EVENT line

name: emit-sub-wildcard-receives-any-event
description: A wildcard SUB (SUB b *) receives events of any event name in the bucket

name: emit-sub-buffered-replay
description: An event EMITted before any subscriber is replayed from the per-event ring buffer when a SUB arrives

name: emit-defaults-event-and-data
description: EMIT with only a bucket defaults event to 'default' and data to null; a wildcard subscriber sees it

name: unsub-stops-delivery
description: After UNSUB, a subsequent EMIT is NOT delivered to the (former) subscriber; asserted as silence within the drain window

name: set-get-string-roundtrip
description: SET stores a JSON string token verbatim; GET returns it so JSON.parse recovers the string

name: set-get-number-roundtrip
description: SET stores a JSON number token verbatim; GET returns it

name: set-get-boolean-roundtrip
description: SET stores a JSON boolean token verbatim; GET returns it

name: set-get-null-roundtrip
description: SET stores the JSON null token verbatim; GET returns it (distinct from NIL, which means key-unset)

name: set-get-object-roundtrip
description: SET stores a JSON object token verbatim; GET returns it so JSON.parse recovers it

name: set-get-array-roundtrip
description: SET stores a JSON array token verbatim; GET returns it

name: get-unset-key-returns-nil
description: GET on a key that was never SET returns NIL

name: set-replies-ok
description: SET replies OK on success

name: watch-fires-current-then-on-set
description: WATCH fires immediately with the current value, then again on every SET

name: watch-unset-key-no-immediate-then-fires-on-set
description: WATCH on an unset key emits no immediate value; the first VALUE arrives on the first SET

name: unwatch-stops-delivery
description: After UNWATCH, a subsequent SET does NOT deliver a VALUE to the (former) watcher

name: ping-pong
description: PING replies PONG

name: stats-returns-json-object
description: STATS returns OK followed by a JSON object containing buckets/subscriptions/keys/uptime_seconds; counts are process-global so matched by regex

name: buckets-returns-json-array
description: BUCKETS returns OK followed by a JSON array of bucket-name strings

name: error-unknown-command
description: An unrecognized command replies ERROR unknown command

name: error-missing-key-on-set
description: SET with a bucket but no key replies ERROR missing bucket or key

name: error-missing-bucket-on-emit
description: EMIT with no bucket replies ERROR missing bucket

name: error-missing-bucket-on-get
description: GET with a bucket but no key replies ERROR missing bucket or key

name: error-oversized-payload
description: A SET whose value exceeds max_payload_bytes (default 262144) replies ERROR payload too large. The value below is a JSON string of 300000 filler characters (well over the default limit) so the vector needs no special config.

name: framing-escaped-newline-roundtrips
description: A SET whose JSON string value contains an escaped newline (the two characters backslash-n) round-trips intact, proving escaped newlines are transport-safe. A LITERAL newline is not representable in one line (that is the framing rule), so it is intentionally not a vector.
